goal:
Restyle and restructure the existing read-only dashboard to match the committed v2 prototype and design-decisions doc.

CONTEXT
The v1 read-only dashboard already lives in this repo at src/dashboard/reader.ts and src/dashboard/server.ts, with tests in tests/dashboard-*.test.ts and fixtures under tests/fixtures/dashboard/. This task ports the finished v2 visual design onto that existing code. It stays a strictly read-only reader over the existing on-disk run-folder format (state.json + trace.jsonl) — it must NOT change what a run emits.

The authoritative design is committed IN THIS REPO — read both before changing anything:
- docs/design/2026-07-21-dashboard-v2-prototype.html  (the interactive prototype: the exact target layout, the run strip behavior, the per-sprint metrics sub-line, and the HALT_REASONS wording all live here)
- docs/design/2026-07-21-dashboard-v2-design-decisions.md  (the decisions and rationale)
Follow the prototype for structure and wording. Aesthetic/visual fidelity is judged by a human afterward — this task is NOT graded on aesthetics beyond the structural requirements below.

REQUIRED STRUCTURAL ELEMENTS (these are the acceptance criteria)
1. Goal-first header: the repo name leads (basename of state.projectPath or runDir), then the run ID, then a status pill. The one-line goal is lifted VERBATIM from the run's own goal text (state.goal) — its first meaningful line, skipping any YAML frontmatter and a leading "#" heading — never AI-summarized. Show it prominently on the planning state; a small "View goal" link on the other states. Provide a reachable full-goal view rendering the complete state.goal.
2. A coarse, monotonic (forward-only) Plan -> Generate -> Done run strip, full-width under the header. NO per-sprint segments — the three stages are fixed; only the fill/color advances. Colored by outcome: in-progress accent, passed = green ("Done"), halted = amber ("Paused").
3. Stats tiles — Sprint X / N, Elapsed (relabels to Duration when finished), Spend — shown once a plan exists; ABSENT during planning.
4. Sprint cards: title + score (score when done, "best N" when halted), the plan description, the phase pipeline Negotiate -> Generate -> Evaluate -> Decide on the active sprint, and a quiet per-sprint metrics sub-line reading exactly: "<N> negotiation rounds · <N> build attempts · <N> file edits · $<cost>".
   - negotiation rounds = the frozen contractVersion on that sprint's NEGOTIATE trace event (already derived by the v1 reader).
   - build attempts = the count of GENERATE trace events for that sprint. DERIVE IT BY COUNTING GENERATE EVENTS. Do NOT read state.iterations — it is known-stale (initialized to 0 and never updated). Do NOT fix state.iterations — that is harness machinery and is out of scope.
   - file edits = the count of "Edit" entries in the toolCalls[] arrays of that sprint's GENERATE events.
   - cost = the sum of costUsd across that sprint's trace events.
5. A plain-language halt-reason banner on a paused/stopped run that translates the raw code to a sentence, covering ALL StopReason codes (max-iteration, no-progress, dollar-ceiling, wall-clock, usage-limit) PLUS the evaluator-parse-error fault. The StopReason codes are graceful "Paused"; evaluator-parse-error is a "Stopped" fault. Match the exact wording in the prototype's HALT_REASONS map.
6. All four run states must render: planning / live (running) / passed / halted.
7. Retain from the current code: dark mode, mobile-friendly responsive layout, client-side polling fetch with in-place DOM updates (no meta-refresh, no full-page reload), and graceful degradation on a mid-write/corrupt state.json.

SCOPE — INTENT-LEVEL RESTRICTIONS (fences, NOT acceptance criteria)
- No changes outside the dashboard module and its tests. Only src/dashboard/* and tests/dashboard-* (plus tests/fixtures/dashboard/*) may change.
- No changes to trace emission, run.ts, or any harness machinery. In particular do not touch state.iterations — derive build attempts from GENERATE events instead.
- The server stays stdlib-only Node (built-in http/fs/path), polling fetch, and strictly read-only — it never writes/creates/modifies any run-folder or config file; no launch/stop controls, no config editing.
- No new npm dependencies. A stdlib-only markdown/goal renderer is fine; do not add a markdown library.

VERIFICATION (verifier command: npm test — vitest, hermetic, offline, no network)
- Extend the fixture run folders under tests/fixtures/dashboard/ as needed so all four run states are covered — including a halted fixture that carries a real haltReason code and trace events exercising the per-sprint metrics (multiple GENERATE events, "Edit" entries in toolCalls[], per-event costUsd).
- Fetch-level checks start the server on an ephemeral port and assert GET / returns 200 and the served page contains the v2 structural elements: the Plan -> Generate -> Done run strip, the goal-first header with the one-line goal, the per-sprint metrics sub-line (the words "negotiation rounds" / "build attempts" / "file edits"), and — for the halted fixture — the plain-language halt-reason text. The /data endpoint still returns 200 JSON and the page still polls (a fetch/setInterval loop, no meta-refresh).
- The module stays import-safe: importing it binds no port and starts no server; it imports only Node stdlib and reads run-folder files (no heavy harness entrypoint that would pull the Anthropic SDK into the verifier's import closure).
- The full existing suite must remain green.
title: dashboard v2 restyle

sprints.title: Reader v2 derivations: goal line, repo, per-sprint metrics
sprints.description: Extend src/dashboard/reader.ts (the read-only assembler) with the v2-required derived fields, all sourced from existing state.json + trace.jsonl and added to DashboardData without changing any emission. Add: a verbatim one-line-goal extractor that takes state.goal's first meaningful line while skipping YAML frontmatter and a leading '#' heading (never summarized); a repo-name field = basename of state.projectPath or runDir; a coarse forward-only run-stage (Plan/Generate/Done) derived from status/plan-presence for the header strip; and per-sprint metrics on each SprintSummary — buildAttempts = count of GENERATE trace events for that sprint (DERIVE by counting; never read state.iterations), fileEdits = count of 'Edit' entries across that sprint's GENERATE toolCalls[], and costUsd = summed costUsd across that sprint's trace events. Keep the module import-safe (Node stdlib + type-only imports only) and degrade gracefully on corrupt/missing input. Ship with reader-level unit tests in tests/dashboard-reader.test.ts asserting each derivation against fixtures (including sprints with multiple GENERATE events, Edit tool calls, and frontmatter/# goal text), and keep the existing reader suite green.

sprints.title: Render the v2 page structure and halt-reason map
sprints.description: Rewrite renderPage in src/dashboard/server.ts to emit the v2 layout per the committed prototype: a goal-first header (repo name, then run ID, then a status pill), a coarse monotonic Plan -> Generate -> Done full-width run strip colored by outcome (accent in-progress, green 'Done' passed, amber 'Paused' halted), stats tiles (Sprint X/N, Elapsed relabeling to Duration when finished, Spend), and sprint cards with title+score (score when done, 'best N' when halted), the plan description, the Negotiate -> Generate -> Evaluate -> Decide phase pipeline on the active sprint, and the exact per-sprint metrics sub-line '<N> negotiation rounds · <N> build attempts · <N> file edits · $<cost>'. Add a HALT_REASONS map covering ALL StopReason codes (max-iteration, no-progress, dollar-ceiling, wall-clock, usage-limit) as graceful 'Paused' plus evaluator-parse-error as a 'Stopped' fault, with wording matching the prototype, rendered as a plain-language banner when a run is halted. Retain dark mode, responsive layout, the client-side polling fetch loop with in-place DOM updates (no meta-refresh/reload), and graceful degradation on corrupt state. Keep the server stdlib-only and import-safe. Ship with fetch-level tests in tests/dashboard-server.test.ts / dashboard-page.test.ts asserting the strip labels, goal-first header + one-line goal, the metrics sub-line words, and (against an existing/temporary halted run) the plain-language halt text; keep the full existing suite green.

sprints.title: All four run states, full-goal view, and fixtures
sprints.description: Complete state coverage and the reachable full-goal view. Add the missing fixtures under tests/fixtures/dashboard/: a 'planning' run (plan not yet formed — goal prominent, strip at Plan, no stats tiles) and a 'halted' run carrying a real haltReason code with trace events that exercise the per-sprint metrics (multiple GENERATE events, 'Edit' entries in toolCalls[], per-event costUsd) plus a best-so-far score; ensure live (running) and passed remain covered by existing fixtures. Wire per-state rendering so planning shows the prominent verbatim goal with a full-goal link and omits stats tiles, while the other states show a small 'View goal' link; and provide a reachable full-goal view (a route or in-page view) that renders the complete state.goal via a stdlib-only markdown/goal renderer (no markdown library, no new deps). Ship with fetch-level tests asserting: GET / returns 200 for all four states with the correct structural elements per state (planning hides tiles + shows prominent goal; halted shows the halt banner text and 'best N'; passed/live show tiles), /data still returns 200 JSON and the page still polls, the full-goal view renders the complete goal, and the module stays import-safe with no changes outside src/dashboard/* and tests/dashboard-*/fixtures. Keep the entire existing suite green.
